notes: Uses Martin Finke's PolyBLEP algorithm for band-limited waveform synthesis. 9 waveform types available: Sine, Triangle, Saw, Square, Noise, Triangle 2, Square 2, Trapezoid 1, Trapezoid 2. PolyBLEP falls back to sine when frequency >= sampleRate/4 to prevent aliasing artifacts. Osc1 writes to voiceBuffer[0], osc2 writes to voiceBuffer[1] during generation - these are NOT stereo channels but separate oscillator outputs that get mixed and panned into stereo afterward. Mix crossfade is linear: osc1 *= (1 - mix), osc2 *= mix. Pan uses BalanceCalculator with equal-power panning law (sqrt(2) * cos/sin). Hard sync resets osc2 phase when osc1 completes a cycle via PolyBLEP::inc() return value. Osc2PitchChain supports audio-rate modulation, enabling FM-style synthesis. The pitch calculation combines octave transpose (pow(2, octaves)), semitone transpose (pow(2, semitones/12)), and detune in cents (pow(2, cents/1200)). MixChain is clamped 0-1, allows audio-rate modulation, and includes monophonic values in voice rendering. When EnableSecondOscillator is off, osc1 output is copied to both channels and mix/pan stages are skipped entirely.

condition.whenTrue: Independent oscillator with separate waveform, pitch, and pulse width
condition.whenFalse: Disabled, osc1 output copied to both internal channels

nodes.label: Pitch Mod
nodes.detail: Inherited pitch chain, applies to both oscillators

nodes.label: Osc2 Pitch Mod
nodes.detail: Independent pitch chain, audio rate, PitchMode

nodes.label: Gain Mod
nodes.detail: Inherited gain chain, applied to both channels before mix

nodes.label: Pan / Balance
nodes.detail: Equal-power panning per oscillator, then summed to stereo

nodes.label: Output
nodes.detail: Stereo, per-voice, summed across voices by ModulatorSynth
